description: '''Turbo-folk''' ({{lang-sr-Cyrl|турбо-фолк}}) is a subgenre of contemporary [[South Slavs|South Slavic]] pop music that initially developed in [[Serbia]] during the 1990s as a fusion of [[techno]] and [[folk music|folk]]. The term "turbo-folk" was coined by [[Montenegrins|Montenegrin]] singer [[Rambo Amadeus]], who jokingly used it to describe an aggressive, satirical style of music. While primarily associated with Serbia, this genre is also popular in other former Yugoslav nations, particularly [[Croatia]] and [[Bosnia and Herzegovina]].
